ops.title: keyhelper cacheDropkey always exits 0 — masks real SecItemDelete failures
ops.description:
The signed Swift keyhelper (helper/Sources/cookiesync-keyhelper/main.swift, cacheDropkey ~line 459) discards SecItemDelete's OSStatus and always exits 0, even on a genuine deletion failure (keychain locked, unexpected SecItem error). Idempotency (errSecItemNotFound => success) is correct, but a REAL failure is masked.

Impact: makes the v0.10.5 Go-side fix (SecureEnclaveWrapper.Close now surfaces a nonzero cache-dropkey Result.Code, cc-notes c177483) INERT end-to-end — the helper never emits a nonzero drop code, so healingWrapper.Close always sees success and clears se even if the Enclave key deletion actually failed, leaving a live key while reporting a clean shutdown. The Go fix is forward-compatible; this is the missing helper half.

Fix: in cacheDropkey, treat errSecSuccess and errSecItemNotFound as exit 0; for any other OSStatus, emit the status on stderr and exit nonzero (use the existing failSec path). Update the documented "exits 0 even when the key is already gone" contract to "exits 0 on success or already-gone; nonzero on a real deletion failure".

Caveat: requires a Developer-ID re-sign + notarize + keyhelper cask re-release (separate pipeline from cookiesync), so it was intentionally out of scope for the v0.10.5 cookiesync patch. Surfaced by the gpt-5.6-sol round-2 review of the v0.10.5 diff.
